title: UN calls for solidarity and political will to stamp out racial discrimination
content: Nearly 70 years after South African police fired upon peaceful demonstrators in Sharpeville protesting apartheid-era laws, killing 69, the UN renewed the commitment to work for justice and equality on Monday, marking the International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: Dire fertiliser shortage a lurking threat due to Hormuz crisis
content: Since the start of the Middle East conflict with Israeli and US strikes on Iran on 28 February, concerns have been growing over rising oil and commodity prices.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: A city opens its doors to the world: New York and the United Nations
content: Born from the ashes of the Second World War, the dream of a more peaceful and fairer world drew nations together in 1945 under a new vision for humanity: the United Nations.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: When justice fails: Why women can’t get protection from AI deepfake abuse
content: She woke up to messages flooding her phone. Doctored images of her, sexualised and viral, had spread while she slept.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: A former refugee takes on a world in flight
content: Barham Salih has spent much of his life crossing borders, first as a young Kurdish exile fleeing repression in Iraq, and now as the UN top official for refugees, confronting a world in which more than 117 million people have been forced from their homes.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: UN mourns constitutional lawyer-turned top crisis diplomat, Nicholas Haysom
content: The UN is mourning the loss of Nicholas Haysom, the Secretary-General’s Special Representative for South Sudan, who went from advising Nelson Mandela following the historic anti-apartheid struggle to becoming a “tireless” champion of crisis diplomacy for the UN in Africa, Asia and the Middle East.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: World News in Brief: Yemen appeal, Middle East war roils Somalia, needs grow in Colombia
content: UN aid teams in Yemen on Wednesday warned that the situation in the war-torn nation remains dire in a call for $2 billion in aid to deliver lifesaving assistance for 12 million people.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: World News in Brief: West Bank displacement, Cuba fuel crisis, sexual abuse safeguards, new ‘humancentric’ AI advocate
content: A new UN human rights report warns that expanding Israeli settlements are driving large-scale displacement of Palestinians across the occupied West Bank.

title: World News in Brief: Attacks on synagogues, Syria’s continuing rights violations, shocking abuse of women during childbirth
content: The first two weeks of March have seen a surge in violent attacks and acts of intimidation targeting synagogues and Jewish institutions across North America and Europe.

title: World News in Brief: Deadly drone attacks in Sudan, violence against women in the spotlight, more clashes in DR Congo
content: UN human rights chief Volker Türk said on Thursday he was appalled by the devastating impact on civilians of increasing drone attacks in Sudan, amid reports that more than 200 civilians have been killed by drones since 4 March alone, in the Kordofan region and White Nile state.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: With Middle East ‘pushed to breaking point’, UN chief urges ceasefire, return to diplomacy
content: The UN chief on Thursday urged all combatants to cease hostilities in the Middle East, uphold international law, protect civilians and make an immediate return to the negotiating table.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: An ‘alarming’ two-thirds of children say cyberbullying has increased
content: Around two-thirds of children worldwide report an increase in cyberbullying, with one in two say they don’t know how to get the right support, according to a recent poll carried out by the UN’s top official who works to end violence against children.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: World News in Brief: Türk’s South Sudan ceasefire call, Ukraine strikes, Gaza food alert, Afghan returnees
content: UN human rights chief Volker Türk on Tuesday condemned accounts of “gruesome killings” of civilians in South Sudan, the destruction and poisoning of key water sources and fresh waves of mass displacement.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: ‘You are the foundation-shakers’: UN chief answers tough questions at townhall with women’s civil society groups
content: The UN chief on Tuesday applauded civil society groups for “shaking the foundations of privilege” in a male-dominated world, addressing a range of questions during a townhall meeting on the margins of the 70th session of the Commission on the Status of Women (CSW70) at UN Headquarters.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: At UN, Malala, Anne Hathaway call for action on women’s rights
content: A Nobel laureate who survived an assassination attempt, a Hollywood actor turned UN advocate and a young Afghan musician whose voice defied repression took the stage at the United Nations on International Women’s Day, delivering a powerful message: justice for women and girls cannot wait.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: UN commission opens with focus on justice for women and girls
content: Access to justice for women and girls dominated the agenda on Monday as the Commission on the Status of Women opened its 70th session at UN Headquarters in New York. Government officials, civil society representatives and UN leaders called for renewed efforts to dismantle the discrimination and legal obstacles that continue to limit women’s and girls’ rights worldwide.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: New challenges bring increased risks in combating child sexual abuse, exploitation
content: Despite some progress made globally, children around the world still face serious and evolving risks of being sold, sexually exploited and abused, the UN independent human rights expert on the sale, sexual exploitation and sexual abuse of children, Mama Fatima Singhateh, warned on Monday.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: ‘No one should be a hibakusha’: Young Japanese activist’s mission to share atomic bomb survivor stories
content: A Japanese peace activist is hoping to promote global nuclear disarmament by keeping alive the stories of the atomic bomb survivors through the creation of a mobile museum.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: LIVE: International Women’s Day and CSW70 at the United Nations
content: Welcome to our live coverage of International Women’s Day 2026 and the opening of the 70th session of the Commission on the Status of Women at UN Headquarters in New York. Throughout the day, we will bring you updates, reactions and key moments from global leaders, diplomats and advocates gathering at the UN, alongside stories and reports from the field across the UN system, as communities around the world mark International Women's Day and advance the theme “Rights. Justice. Action” for all women and girls. UN News app users can follow the coverage here.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: Justice takes centre stage: UN welcomes world’s largest women’s rights event
content: Women have never been closer to equality and never closer to losing it, according to UN Women ahead of the start of the world’s largest gender equality gathering.

title: Women’s representation in parliament sees sluggish gains
content: Women held 27.5 per cent of parliamentary seats worldwide as the year began –a 0.3 per cent increase from 2025, marking the slowest growth in nearly a decade, according to a new report from the UN-backed Inter-Parliamentary Union (IPU).
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: World News In Brief: Sudan conflict intensifies, Global inequality deepening, HIV success amid new medicine, increase in ‘Domicide’ worldwide
content: There has been an alarming escalation of violence in the Kordofan region where fighting has killed and injured civilians, destroyed homes, hospitals and other civilian infrastructure.

title: UN calls for fair play in the global race for critical minerals
content: Critical minerals such as lithium and cobalt are central to the transition to a zero-carbon economy. As the Security Council meets on Thursday to discuss “energy, critical minerals and security,” here is some of the work the UN is doing to ensure that the transition is just and equitable.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories

title: How AI is already reshaping working conditions
content: From delivery couriers compelled to follow the demands of online platform algorithms to content moderators who confront pornography and death every day while training artificial intelligence (AI) systems, the impact of new technologies on working conditions is becoming increasingly obvious.
sourceTitle: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
sourceDescription: UN News - Global perspective Human stories | Global

title: ‘The world is looking to you for clarity’, UN chief tells AI experts
content: Secretary-General António Guterres told the inaugural meeting of a new independent group of experts on Artificial Intelligence convened by the UN that they have a huge responsibility to help shape how it is used “for the benefit of humanity”.

title: Guterres slams international failure to manage safe migration
content: A 2018 agreement that aims to strengthen international cooperation on migration management must become reality, the UN Secretary-General said on Friday in New York.
